raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2234 modernizes the framework for homebuilders and local governments to process post-entitlement residential building permit applications more efficiently and cost-effectively.",
    "The process of applying for and receiving post-entitlement permits can be confusing and unpredictable under current law.",
    "The confusion in the permitting process drives up the cost of new housing.",
    "Affordable housing developments often rely on grant funding that may expire if building permits are not returned in time.",
    "California faces a significant housing shortage of almost 3 million homes.",
    "AB 2234 enhances transparency, accountability, and communication between city governments and home builders.",
    "The bill aims to ensure that housing for residents of all income levels moves forward more efficiently.",
    "AB 2234 signifies a new approach to addressing California’s housing crisis through collaboration between businesses, legislative leaders, housing advocates, and developers.",
    "Streamlining the post-entitlement permitting process is intended to improve efficiency, predictability, and accountability.",
    "Standardizing the building permit process for local governments is proposed as a solution to the housing crisis in California."
  ]
}
```
